title: Chipotle Chicken Tortas
description: A high-protein torta with grilled spiced chicken breast, smoky chipotle yogurt sauce, and creamy black beans on a toasted whole wheat bun.
ingredients: 700g (1.5 lbs) chicken breast, sliced thin or butterflied
4 whole wheat sandwich thin buns or light hamburger buns (~100 cal each), split and toasted
1½ tbsp chipotle hot sauce (adjust to taste)
1 tsp lemon juice / 1 tsp bottled lemon juice
3 cups cooked black beans (or two 15 oz cans, drained and rinsed)
Juice of 1 lime (about 2 tbsp lime juice)
instructions: BRINE THE CHICKEN: Dissolve 2 tbsp salt in 4 cups cold water. Submerge chicken breasts and brine for 20–30 minutes. Rinse and pat dry.

MAKE THE SAUCE: Combine 1 cup plain 0% Greek yogurt, 1½ tbsp chipotle hot sauce, 1 tbsp ground chia seeds, 1 tsp lemon juice, ½ tsp garlic powder, and a pinch of salt in a bowl. Stir well, taste, and adjust heat. Refrigerate until needed — it will thicken as it sits.

MAKE THE BEANS: Heat 1 tsp canola oil in a large pan over medium heat. Sauté 1 yellow onion, chopped, until golden, about 5 minutes. Add 3 cloves minced garlic, 1 tbsp chili powder, and ¾ tsp cumin; cook 30 seconds. Add 3 cups black beans and 1 cup broth, stir and bring to a simmer. Reduce heat and mash beans with a spoon or masher to a chunky texture. Add juice of 1 lime (about 2 tbsp), taste, and adjust seasoning.

SEASON AND COOK THE CHICKEN: Combine 1 tsp chili powder, 1 tsp garlic powder, ½ tsp cumin, ½ tsp smoked paprika, ½ tsp salt, and ¼ tsp black pepper. Coat brined chicken evenly. Grill or air fry at 400°F (200°C) for 12–15 minutes, flipping halfway, until internal temp hits 165°F (74°C). Rest 5 minutes, then slice thin.

BUILD THE TORTA: Spread a generous spoonful of chipotle yogurt sauce on both halves of the toasted bun. Layer sliced chicken, a big scoop of black beans, lettuce, tomato, red onion, and ¼ avocado. Serve immediately.
notes: Black beans contribute meaningful protein but also significant carbs — if you want to tighten carbs further, reduce beans to ½ cup per torta and add more chicken. The yogurt sauce keeps in the fridge 3–4 days and works well as a dip or salad dressing. Bun choice is the biggest carb lever in this recipe.
